source: .specs/agent-scenarios.md → Feature: Multiple sessions in same cwd
reason: The codex interactive driver (scripts/drive-codex-interactive.sh) launches exactly one tmux/codex process per run; every step type operates on a single $SESSION. There is no parallel_session / tmux_split primitive to spawn a SECOND concurrent codex in the same cwd while the first stays alive and route send/wait_turn to a named target. The driver's session.uuids/transcript.paths lists capture SERIAL /clear+/fork session swaps within one process, not two concurrently-live processes — so the PID-cwd disambiguation this scenario exercises (two simultaneous rollouts the daemon must split by DiscoverPIDByTranscriptWriter) cannot be elicited as-shipped. Agent capability and irrlicht observability are both fine (assessment.json: agent_supports=yes, irrlicht_observes=yes, confidence 0.9); only the recording rig is missing. Same blocker as the claudecode cell.
notes: Spec preserved for forward compatibility. Future phases (once the driver gains a parallel_session / tmux_split step that can route send/wait_turn to a named target): sessionA_birth (ready, new_session) and sessionB_birth (ready, new_session, distinct rollout UUID); sessionA_pid_bind / sessionB_pid_bind (pid_discovered per session, distinct PIDs — codex binds via DiscoverPIDByTranscriptWriter, the PID holding each rollout open); sessionA_turn (ready→working→ready, same_session_as sessionA_birth) and sessionB_turn (ready→working→ready, same_session_as sessionB_birth) driven independently; invariants on both turn windows: no state_transition cross-contamination between rows, PID-to-session binding stable. Two DISTINCT rollout UUIDs (codex mints its own — session_id_assignment:false, cannot pre-pin) must appear in events.jsonl with their own arcs and their own pid_discovered events. Driver extension blocked: needs a concurrent-second-session primitive for codex.
